title: Cryptocurrency Market Microstructure

analysis.object: T236: Cryptocurrency market microstructure depth analysis

order_book.description: Limit order book: list of bid/ask prices and sizes
order_book.reason: Discrete list of orders — pure combinatorics

price_log_normal.description: Log price follows Brownian motion: d(ln P) = μdt + σdW

mean_reversion.description: Market making: oscillatory buy/sell around mid-price

flash_crash.description: Millisecond price collapse: cascade of liquidations
flash_crash.reason: Phase transition: order book collapses discontinuously

analysis.predictability_classification: Does the framework classify which market phenomena are predictable vs EML-∞? YES. EML-2 (log-normal): predictable with Gaussian models. EML-3 (mean reversion): predictable with oscillatory models (Ornstein-Uhlenbeck). EML-∞ (flash crash): structurally unpredictable — cross-type product. Flash crash = EML-3 (market microstructure oscillation) × EML-2 (log-normal price) = EML-∞. Same superspreader mechanism: cross-type interaction → unpredictability.

analysis.verdict: Crypto: log-price EML-2, mean-reversion EML-3, flash crash EML-∞ cross-type.
analysis.theorem: T236: Crypto Market Depth — flash crash = EML-3×EML-2 = EML-∞ superspreader

key_theorem: T237: Crypto Market Depth (S515). Log price: EML-2 (log-normal Brownian). Mean reversion: EML-3. Flash crash: EML-3 × EML-2 = EML-∞ (superspreader cross-type). AMM constant product: EML-2 (hyperbola). Predictability: EML-2 (Gaussian) and EML-3 (OU) predictable; EML-∞ not.
rabbit_hole_log: d(ln P) = μdt + σdW → EML-2 log-normal
T236: Flash crash = superspreader, same cross-type mechanism
